concept.title: DITA Authoring, Review and Governance
concept.shortdesc: This section answers questions about the metR's authoring and review process.

section.title: What kind of training will our writing team need to adopt DITA authoring?
section.p: Most teams require lightweight DITA enablement, not deep XML training. Writers can start by reviewing converted DITA topics, making guided edits in metR’s DocEditor, and gradually learning core DITA concepts such as topic types (concept, task, reference), reuse, and metadata. Advanced XML editors like oXygen are optional and recommended only for teams doing heavy customization or complex authoring.

section.title: How is the content review process handled after conversion to DITA?
section.p: metR enables post-conversion review using Git-light governance and block-level diffs, making changes easy to review and approve. By combining these tools with defined review and approval workflows, teams can maintain clear traceability of content changes to support audit and compliance requirements.

section.title: Can users customize the output DITA structure to match their organization's standards?

p.b: Yes. metR supports custom content models, metadata structures, and DITA-OT plugins.

section.p: Organizations can align topic structures, attributes, and publishing rules with internal standards using custom mappings, plugins, and governance rules configured during onboarding or enterprise deployments.
